docs.title: A Multi-Agent System for Interdisciplinary Research
docs.text: OnePersonLab-Agents enables a single researcher to coordinate an entire virtual laboratory of AI-powered agents, each specializing in different scientific disciplines.

docs.title: 1. Install Universal Skills
docs.text:
# Install required skills
clawhub install memory-setup
clawhub install tavily-search
clawhub install academic-research-hub
clawhub install self-improving
clawhub install find-skills
clawhub install nano-pdf

docs.title: 2. Start a Conversation
docs.text:
You are now the Lab-Director of my OnePersonLab.
Based on my research goal, please configure and activate the required agent team.

docs.title: 3. Describe Your Research
docs.text:
I want to build an AI-driven drug discovery platform.
I need expertise in chemistry, biology, and machine learning.

That's it! Your research team is assembled and ready.

docs.title: Universal Skills (Required)
docs.text:
All agents have these core capabilities:

Skill Purpose memory-setup Persistent memory and context tavily-search Web search for current information academic-research-hub Academic paper search self-improving Self-reflection and learning find-skills Discover new skills nano-pdf PDF reading and editing

@software{onepersonlab2026,
  title = {OnePersonLab-Agents: A Multi-Agent System for Interdisciplinary Research},
  author = {OnePersonLab Team},

docs.title: BIO-PI: Principal Investigator
docs.text:
Specialties: Molecular Biology, Cell Biology, Computational Biology, Synthetic Biology, Genetics, Ecology, Bioinformatics, Physiology

Supervises: 8 Postdocs, 17 Students

docs.title: Postdocs
docs.text: ID Specialty Students BIO-PD-MOL Molecular Biology, Gene Editing 2 BIO-PD-CELL Cell Biology, Stem Cells 3 BIO-PD-COMP Computational Biology, Bioinformatics 2 BIO-PD-SYN Synthetic Biology, Metabolic Engineering 2 BIO-PD-GEN Genetics, Genomics 2 BIO-PD-BIOINF Bioinformatics, Computational Genomics 2 BIO-PD-ECOL Ecology, Conservation Biology 2 BIO-PD-PHYS Physiology, Systems Biology 2

docs.title: Hierarchical Structure
docs.text:
OnePersonLab-Agents uses a three-tier hierarchy:

Lab-Director → PI → Postdoc → Students

docs.title: Lab-Director
docs.text:
Role: Central coordinator for the entire virtual laboratory.

Responsibilities: - Activate appropriate agent combinations - Route tasks to suitable PIs - Coordinate cross-disciplinary collaboration - Track progress across all agents - Manage shared resource pool

Key Feature: Skills configuration is a priority task before any project begins.

docs.title: Principal Investigators (PIs)
docs.text:
Each discipline has one PI leading the research team:

PI ID Discipline Postdocs Students CS-PI Computer Science 8 17 CHEM-PI Chemistry 8 16 BIO-PI Biology 8 17 MAT-PI Materials Science 6 12 ENV-PI Environmental Science 7 14 AGR-PI Agriculture 7 14 ENG-PI Engineering 8 14 MED-PI Medicine 7 13 ECON-PI Economics 6 12 PSYCH-PI Psychology 7 12 DS-PI Data Science 6 12

docs.title: Postdocs
docs.text:
Each postdoc specializes in a specific research area and mentors 2-4 students.

Example: BIO-PD-MOL (Molecular Biology) - Specialty: Molecular Biology, Gene Editing - Students: BIO-GR-01-GENO, BIO-GR-02-PROT - Research Focus: CRISPR systems, epigenetic modifications

docs.title: Students
docs.text:
Graduate students (PhD/Master) working on specific research topics under postdoc mentorship.

Ratio: Average 2 students per postdoc (range: 2-4)

docs.title: AI-Driven Drug Discovery
docs.text:
Disciplines: Chemistry + Biology + Computer Science

Team: - CHEM-PI, BIO-PI, CS-PI (Co-leads) - CHEM-PD-ORG, BIO-PD-MOL, CS-PD-AI (Postdocs) - Multiple students from each discipline

Timeline: 15 days

View Full Example →

docs.title: MatBench Discovery Optimization
docs.text:
Discipline: Materials Science + Computer Science

Goal: Develop ML models for crystal stability prediction

Benchmark: MatBench Discovery (Nature Machine Intelligence 2025)

Target Venue: Nature Machine Intelligence / npj Computational Materials

View Research Plan →

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text:
1. Choose a project type (single or cross-discipline)
  2. Install universal skills
  3. Activate your team via Lab-Director
  4. Start research!

Back to Home

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Cross-discipline collaboration between Chemistry, Biology, and Computer Science.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
project:
  name: "AI-Driven Drug Discovery Platform"
  type: cross-discipline
  lead_pi: CHEM-PI
  co_pi:
    - BIO-PI
    - CS-PI
  members:
    - CHEM-PD-ORG
    - BIO-PD-MOL
    - CS-PD-AI
    - CHEM-GR-03-CATAL
    - BIO-GR-01-GENO
    - CS-GR-02-CV
  timeline: 15-days
  budget: balanced

docs.title: Day 0: Skills Check
docs.text:
• Verify universal skills for all agents
  • Discuss project-specific skill needs

docs.title: Day 1-3: Platform Setup
docs.text:
• CS Team: Build molecular generation model
  • CHEM Team: Define target chemical space
  • BIO Team: Identify therapeutic targets

docs.title: Day 4-8: Candidate Generation
docs.text:
• Generate candidate molecules
  • Evaluate synthetic feasibility
  • Assess target binding predictions

docs.title: Day 9-12: Analysis and Refinement
docs.text:
• Joint analysis of candidates
  • Provide synthesis feedback
  • Refine target selection

docs.title: Day 13-15: Documentation
docs.text:
• Write project report
  • Prepare paper draft

docs.title: Skills Used
docs.text:
Universal (All Agents): - memory-setup, tavily-search, academic-research-hub - self-improving, find-skills, nano-pdf

Discipline-Specific (Examples): - Chemistry: cheminformatics, molecular-modeling - Biology: genomics-tools, pathway-analysis - CS: deep-learning-frameworks, molecular-generation

docs.title: Success Metrics
docs.text:
• Platform generates valid molecules ✓
  • At least 20 candidates pass feasibility filter ✓
  • Project report completed in 15 days ✓
  • Paper draft ready for submission ✓

Back to Examples

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: MatBench Discovery (Nature Machine Intelligence 2025) is a framework for evaluating ML models in crystal stability prediction.

docs.title: Key Metrics
docs.text: Metric Symbol Direction Combined Performance Score CPS ↑ Higher is better Discovery Acceleration Factor DAF ↑ Higher is better F1 Score F1 ↑ Higher is better Mean Absolute Error MAE ↓ Lower is better

docs.title: Current SOTA (2026)
docs.text: Model CPS DAF Params PET-OAM-XL 0.898 6.075 730M eSEN-30M-OAM 0.888 6.069 30.2M EquFlash 0.888 5.983 28.7M

docs.title: Research Directions
docs.text:
1. Efficient Model Architecture
  2. Can we achieve SOTA with <10M parameters?

  3. Model compression/distillation

  4. Data Strategy Optimization

  5. Quality vs quantity tradeoff

  6. Active learning for DFT reduction

  7. Dynamic Benchmark

  8. Continuously updated test sets
  9. Community-driven data contribution

docs.title: Resources
docs.text:
• Official Site: https://matbench-discovery.materialsproject.org/
  • Paper: https://www.nature.com/articles/s42256-025-01055-1
  • GitHub: https://github.com/janosh/matbench-discovery

Back to Examples

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: A single-discipline project within Computer Science to optimize transformer models for efficiency.

docs.title: ⚠️ Prerequisites: Skills Setup
docs.text:
# Install universal skills
clawhub install memory-setup
clawhub install tavily-search
clawhub install academic-research-hub
clawhub install self-improving
clawhub install find-skills
clawhub install nano-pdf

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
project:
  name: "Efficient Transformer Optimization"
  type: single-discipline
  lead_pi: CS-PI
  members:
    - CS-PD-AI
    - CS-GR-01-NLP
    - CS-GR-03-RL
  timeline: 2-months
  budget: compute-heavy

docs.title: Day 0: Skills Check
docs.text:
• Lab-Director: Verify universal skills installed
  • User + Lab-Director: Discuss if additional skills needed

docs.title: Day 1-3: Literature Review
docs.text:
• CS-GR-01-NLP: Survey efficient attention mechanisms
  • Deliverable: Survey document

docs.title: Day 4-7: Baseline Implementation
docs.text:
• Implement baseline transformer
  • Set up RL environment

docs.title: Day 8-12: Optimization Experiments
docs.text:
• Test pruning strategies
  • Apply RL for architecture search

docs.title: Day 13-15: Analysis and Writing
docs.text:
• Analyze results
  • Write paper draft

docs.title: Skills Used
docs.text:
• memory-setup: Track project progress
  • academic-research-hub: Literature search
  • tavily-search: Find code repositories
  • nano-pdf: Read PDF papers
  • find-skills: Discover NLP-specific tools

docs.title: Success Metrics
docs.text:
• 2x speedup over baseline
  • <1% accuracy drop
  • Paper submitted to target venue

Back to Examples

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: This document defines the universal skills that all OnePersonLab agents must have installed.

docs.title: Universal Skills (Required)
docs.text: Skill Purpose memory-setup Persistent memory and context tavily-search Web search academic-research-hub Academic paper search self-improving Self-reflection find-skills Discover new skills nano-pdf PDF reading/editing

docs.text:
clawhub install memory-setup
clawhub install tavily-search
clawhub install academic-research-hub
clawhub install self-improving
clawhub install find-skills
clawhub install nano-pdf

docs.text:
Documented in skills_specific.md

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: All OnePersonLab agents have 6 universal skills installed by default.

docs.title: Skills List
docs.text: Skill Purpose Configuration memory-setup Persistent memory and context ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json tavily-search Web search for current information API key required academic-research-hub Academic paper search API keys for services self-improving Self-reflection and learning Automatic find-skills Discover new skills Automatic nano-pdf PDF reading and editing Automatic

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
# Install all universal skills
clawhub install memory-setup
clawhub install tavily-search
clawhub install academic-research-hub
clawhub install self-improving
clawhub install find-skills
clawhub install nano-pdf

# Verify installation
clawhub list

docs.title: memory-setup
docs.text:
Enable in ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json:

{
  "agents": {
    "defaults": {
      "memorySearch": {
        "enabled": true,
        "provider": "local",
        "sources": ["memory", "sessions"]
      }
    }
  }
}

docs.title: tavily-search
docs.text: export TAVILY_API_KEY="your-api-key"

docs.title: Discipline-Specific Skills
docs.text:
Discovered via find-skills based on project needs:

"Find skills for [your task description]"

Examples: - "Find skills for RNA-seq analysis" - "Find skills for molecular generation" - "Find skills for climate modeling"

docs.text:
